stocks.name: Rigetti Computing, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Rigetti surged after the U.S. Department of Commerce announced a $100 million CHIPS Act grant to the quantum computing company as part of a broader $2 billion quantum investment package. The government will take a minority equity stake. Analysts at Cantor Fitzgerald and Craig-Hallum reiterated Buy ratings with $30 price targets.

stocks.name: Lenovo Group Limited
stocks.explanation: Lenovo posted record full-year revenue of $83.1 billion, with AI-related sales growing 105% for the year. Fourth-quarter revenue hit $21.6 billion, up 27% year-over-year, with AI accounting for 38% of quarterly revenue. The company is targeting $100 billion in revenue within two years.

stocks.name: D-Wave Quantum Inc.
stocks.explanation: D-Wave rallied on the Trump administration's $2 billion quantum computing grant program, with D-Wave slated to receive $100 million under the CHIPS Act. The company will issue $100 million in common stock to the Department of Commerce as part of the arrangement. Total bookings surged nearly 2,000% to a record $33.4 million in Q1.

stocks.name: Dell Technologies Inc.
stocks.explanation: Dell surged to an all-time high after multiple analyst upgrades driven by AI server demand. JPMorgan raised its price target to $280 from $205, Citigroup lifted to $290, and Wells Fargo to $270. The company guided fiscal 2027 revenue of $138-142 billion, representing roughly 23% growth from record fiscal 2026 revenue of $113.5 billion.

stocks.name: Quantum Computing Inc.
stocks.explanation: Quantum Computing Inc. rode the broad quantum sector rally triggered by the U.S. government's $2 billion CHIPS Act quantum investment program. Quantum stocks across the sector surged as investors viewed the federal commitment as validation of the industry's commercial potential, with some names climbing 30% in a single session.

stocks.name: Arqit Quantum Inc.
stocks.explanation: Arqit Quantum surged alongside the broader quantum computing sector following the Department of Commerce's announcement of $2 billion in CHIPS Act grants to nine quantum firms. The government's decision to take equity stakes in recipients signaled long-term federal commitment to the quantum industry.

stocks.name: Qualcomm Incorporated
stocks.explanation: Qualcomm jumped after Stellantis announced a sweeping expansion of its multi-year partnership to integrate Snapdragon Digital Chassis chips across cockpit, connectivity, and ADAS systems for its entire next-generation vehicle portfolio. The deal validates Qualcomm's automotive diversification strategy as the company pivots beyond handset dependency.

stocks.name: Zoom Communications, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Zoom beat Q1 expectations with EPS of $1.55 versus $1.42 consensus and revenue of $1.24 billion, up 5.5% year-over-year. AI Companion paid users grew 184% year-over-year as the company positions itself as an AI-first platform. Management raised full-year guidance and boosted its share repurchase plan by $1 billion.

stocks.name: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company
stocks.explanation: HPE hit an all-time high amid continued AI infrastructure enthusiasm. JPMorgan raised its price target to $37 from $27 and Evercore ISI lifted to $40 from $30, reflecting strong investor confidence in HPE's data center and AI server positioning. The stock has surged 98% over the past year.

stocks.name: IonQ, Inc.
stocks.explanation: IonQ rallied alongside quantum peers following the $2 billion federal quantum grant program, even though it was not a direct recipient. The company reported record Q1 revenue of $64.7 million, up 755% year-over-year, and raised full-year guidance to $260-270 million. Wedbush raised its price target to $75 with an Outperform rating.

stocks.name: ON Semiconductor Corporation
stocks.explanation: ON Semiconductor continued its strong 2026 rally, up nearly 100% year-to-date, driven by rising demand for power semiconductors in AI infrastructure and automotive applications. The company recently completed a $1.3 billion zero-coupon convertible senior notes offering and announced a share buyback pivot.

stocks.name: Spotify Technology S.A.
stocks.explanation: Spotify surged following a landmark licensing deal with Universal Music Group allowing Premium subscribers to create AI-generated covers and remixes of participating artists' catalogues. The opt-in framework based on consent, credit, and compensation was announced at Spotify's Investor Day and opens a new monetization path for the platform.

stocks.name: Super Micro Computer, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Supermicro gained after JPMorgan upgraded the stock alongside Dell amid an IT hardware recovery thesis centered on AI server demand. The upgrade came as enterprise spending on AI infrastructure continues to accelerate, benefiting GPU server assemblers like Supermicro.

stocks.name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Cadence Design Systems rose as the semiconductor EDA sector benefited from AMD's $10 billion Taiwan investment announcement and the UCLA Semiconductor Hub launch backed by Broadcom, Meta, and others. Cadence reported 18.7% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, reflecting strong chip design activity.

stocks.name: Workday, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Workday jumped after posting Q1 fiscal 2027 subscription revenue of $2.455 billion, up 13% year-over-year, and raising its non-GAAP operating margin guidance to 30.5% from 30%. The company cited accelerated AI adoption across product and go-to-market as a key driver of improving margins.
